ASBESTOS DISCOVERY 503, PETITION OF DANIELE MCCOLLUM TO PERPETUATE TESTIMONY OF THOMAS MCCOLLUM BY VIDEOTAPED DEPOSITION IS GRANTED IN PART, AND DENIED IN PART. PETITION IS DENIED AS TO WEATHER SYSTEMS, INC. PETITION IS GRANTED AS TO ALL OTHER POSSIBLE ADVERSE PARTIES. DEPOSITION MUST BE COMPLETED BY MAY-19-2021. PARTIES TO FILE A NOTICE OF APPEARANCE WITHIN 48 HOURS OF RECEIPT OF THE ORDER. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: CYNTHIA MING-MEI LEE, CLERK: A. MOK. REPORTER: LORI ANASTASIOU CSR4345, LORI1C@YAHOO.COM, 714-345-6124. (503)

Notice Of Hearing Re: Petition Of Daniele Mccollum To Perpetuate Testimony Of Thomas Mccollum By Videotaped Deposition
Off Calendar Feb-18-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

RULING:
Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: The Petition of Daniele McCollum to Perpetuate Testimony of Thomas McCollum by Videotaped Deposition is granted in part, and denied in part. The Petition is denied as to Weather Systems, Inc. due to Petitioner's failure to file a proof of service as to this party. (Code Civ. Proc., 2035.040, subd. (a), (c).) The Petition is granted as to all other possible adverse parties identified within paragraph 11 of the Petition. The Petition has met the statutory requirements outlined in Code of Civil Procedure 2035.030, subd. (b). The Petition does not violate Code of Civil Procedure 2035.010, subd. (b). The Petition demonstrates that the discovery requested may prevent a failure or delay of justice. (Code Civ. Proc. 2035.050, subd. (a).) The Court has noted that American International Supply has filed an unredacted version of Mr. McCollum's responses to standard asbestos case interrogatories which display Mr. McCollum's full social security number. Once a document has been filed with the Court, it cannot be "removed" from the docket. In order to seal a portion of this record, the parties should refer to the relevant California Rules of Court, Rules 2.550 and 2.551. Counsel to submit Motion to seal for AIS's Opposition for redaction of plaintiff's information. Mr. McCollum's deposition must be completed by Wednesday, May 19, 2021. Parties to file a notice of appearance within 48 hours of receipt of the order. Order signed in court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Reporter: Lori Anastasiou CSR4345, Lori1c@yahoo.com, 714-345-6124. (503/CML)
